Title: Gibberellin signaling regulates pectin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes late 3β-hydroxylation steps in gibberellin biosynthesis to produce bioactive GA
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell adhesion; wall integrity; seed mucilage production; seedling growth
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies upstream hormone signal that promotes target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pectin biosynthesis indirectly via GA production; ga3ox1-3 reduces mucilage and pectin-associated gene expression, and exogenous GA3 restores the defect.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ga3ox1-3 mutant analysis; exogenous GA3 rescue; seed mucilage staining and quantification; RT-qPCR of pectin biosynthesis genes
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (IP-MS), PXD062288; public microarray datasets GSE12404; GSE20039
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ga3ox1-3 shows reduced seed mucilage and seedling growth defects unless rescued by GA3
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ga3ox1-3 mutants showed significantly reduced seed mucilage and lower expression of mucilage biosynthesis genes. 2. Exogenous GA3 completely restored the mucilage defect, linking GA biosynthesis to pectin production. 3. MYB5 overexpression completely rescued ga3ox1-3 mucilage and growth defects, placing GA3ox1 upstream of the GA-DELLA-MBW-TTG2 module.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GA3ox1 is not the main mechanistic focus but is experimentally used as a core upstream GA-biosynthetic determinant connecting endogenous GA level to pectin output.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating GA biosynthesis could increase pectin-associated wall traits, but likely requires balancing growth and hormone pleiotropy.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated transcriptional repression; competitive inhibition of activator complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: pectin biosynthesis via control of transcriptional activation of GL2 and downstream RG-I biosynthesis genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell adhesion; wall integrity; seed mucilage production; seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Repress pectin biosynthesis; DELLA loss increases mucilage/RG-I and seedling pectin, whereas high DELLA state under low GA reduces pectin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central negative regulators linking GA status to pectin output by directly suppressing both MBW complex function and TTG2 activity.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TTG1; MYB5; TT2; EGL3; TT8; TTG2; GL2 (indirect transcriptional output); MUM4; GATL5; RRT1; URGT2 (downstream)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Inhibit activation of GL2 by disrupting MBW assembly and TTG2-mediated activation, thereby reducing downstream pectin biosynthetic gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: gibberellin (GA); GA3 application; PAC-mediated GA depletion
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Act on MBW complex and TTG2-associated activator complexes; competitively hinder TTG2-MYB5/EGL3/TTG1 and TTG1-MYB5/EGL3 interactions
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TTG1; MYB5; TT2; EGL3; TT8; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression negatively correlates with mucilage synthesis genes during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in developing siliques and seedlings; DELLA expression increased in ttg1-1 seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DELLA mutant series (dellaD/dellaQ/dellaP; ga1-3 rga-2; ga1-3 dellaD); GA3/PAC treatments; Y2H; split-LUC; co-IP; Y3H; ChIP-qPCR; dual-LUC; genetic epistasis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3 application; PAC treatment; dark-grown hypocotyl assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (IP-MS), PXD062288; public microarray datasets GSE12404; GSE20039
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High DELLA activity associates with reduced mucilage, reduced pectin, stunted growth, and cell-adhesion defects; DELLA removal enhances growth and pectin.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DELLAs are partially redundant repressors of GA-mediated pectin biosynthesis, with RGA and GAI having dominant roles. 2. DELLAs physically interact with MBW proteins and TTG2, weakening their interactions, promoter binding, and GL2 activation. 3. DELLA depletion increases RG-I-rich mucilage and seedling pectin, promoting growth and cell adhesion.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTG2 is epistatic to DELLAs in seedling growth and cell adhesion, whereas TTG1 showed a more complex relationship in seedlings, possibly also affecting DELLA expression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing DELLA repression could enhance pectin-associated cell-wall traits and growth, but GA/DELLA pleiotropy must be carefully managed.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pTTG2 for complementation/ChIP; 35S in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; cooperative activation with MBW proteins; repression by DELLA competitive interaction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: pectin biosynthesis, especially via activation of GL2 and downstream mucilage/pectin genes
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell adhesion; wall integrity; seed mucilage production; seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pectin biosynthesis and GA-responsive mucilage and seedling growth; ttg2 mutation abolishes GA-induced mucilage increase and causes reduced seedling pectin and growth.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator required for GA-mediated pectin biosynthesis and a key downstream executor of the GA-DELLA module.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates GL2 transcription via promoter binding; synergizes with MYB5 and EGL3 to further enhance GL2 activation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms functional activating interactions with MYB5 and EGL3; also interacts with TTG1; these interactions are attenuated by GAI
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GAI; RGA; RGL1; RGL2; RGL3; TTG1; MYB5; EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in developing seed coat and seedlings based on phenotypic and ChIP analyses
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ttg2-1 mutant; ttg2-1 dellaD epistasis; pTTG2:TTG2-GFP ChIP-qPCR; Y2H; split-LUC; co-IP; BiFC; dual-LUC; GA3 treatment assays
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3 application; PAC treatment; dark-grown hypocotyl assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (IP-MS), PXD062288; public microarray datasets GSE12404; GSE20039
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of TTG2 reduces mucilage, pectin content, cotyledon/root/hypocotyl growth, and causes cell-adhesion defects.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG2 is required for GA-induced pectin biosynthesis in seed coat and seedlings. 2. TTG2 directly binds and activates GL2, and this activation is enhanced by MYB5/EGL3 but blocked by DELLAs. 3. ttg2 mutants are epistatic to DELLA loss in several growth and adhesion traits, highlighting TTG2 as a core downstream regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTG2 is both a downstream target of MBW from previous work and an interacting co-activator with MBW proteins in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: TTG2 is a strong engineering target for increasing pectin-related wall properties, though effects on development and cell adhesion should be monitored.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein complex assembly enabling transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: pectin biosynthesis through MBW complex assembly and GL2 activation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell adhesion; wall integrity; seed mucilage production; seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required positive regulator of pectin biosynthesis; ttg1 mutation blocks GA-induced mucilage biosynthesis and reduces seedling pectin and growth.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Representative core MBW component necessary for GA-responsive pectin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports cooperative activation of GL2 with MYB5/EGL3; its interactions are disrupted by DELLA proteins.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible negative effect on DELLA expression in seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core component of MBW complex; interacts with MYB5, EGL3, and TTG2; these interactions are competitively weakened by GAI
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GAI; RGL1; RGL2; RGL3; MYB5; EGL3; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in developing seed coat and seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ttg1-1 mutant; ttg1-1 dellaD epistasis; split-LUC; co-IP; Y3H; dual-LUC; GA3 response assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (IP-MS), PXD062288; public microarray datasets GSE12404; GSE20039
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes severe mucilage defects and impaired growth/pectin traits; seedling epistasis with DELLA differs from seed coat behavior.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 is required for GA-induced mucilage production and seedling growth-associated pectin biosynthesis. 2. DELLA proteins weaken TTG1 interactions within MBW and with TTG2, suppressing GL2 activation. 3. In seedlings, TTG1 may additionally influence DELLA expression, indicating context-dependent network wiring.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although TTG1 is not a DNA-binding TF, it is treated here as a regulatory core gene because it is a primary experimental subject controlling transcriptional complexes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for modulating pectin-related traits, but context-dependent roles in GA signaling and broad developmental pleiotropy warrant caution.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pMYB5 for complementation/ChIP; 35S for overexpression; 35S in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation in MBW and TTG2-associated complexes; repression by DELLA interaction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: pectin biosynthesis via direct activation of GL2 with partner proteins
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell adhesion; wall integrity; seed mucilage production; seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of pectin biosynthesis; MYB5 overexpression increases mucilage and GL2 expression and rescues GA-deficient defects.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive effector linking MBW activity and GA signaling to GL2 activation and pectin output.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds the GL2 promoter and activates its expression cooperatively with TTG1/EGL3 or TTG2; DELLA accumulation reduces promoter binding and transcriptional activation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Participates in MBW complex and interacts with TTG2; both activities are inhibited by DELLAs
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RGA; GAI; RGL1; RGL2; RGL3; TTG1; EGL3; TT2; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in developing siliques and seedlings; myb5 single mutant has clear seed mucilage but limited seedling phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: myb5-2 mutant; pMYB5:MYB5-GFP complementation; ChIP-qPCR; split-LUC; co-IP; IP-MS; dual-LUC; 35S:MYB5 overexpression; rescue of ga1-3 and ga3ox1-3
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3 application; PAC treatment; dark-grown hypocotyl assay
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (IP-MS), PXD062288; public microarray datasets GSE12404; GSE20039
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression alters wall/metabolic output and development; endogenous redundancy may complicate effects across tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB5 directly associates with the GL2 promoter and positively regulates pectin biosynthesis. 2. DELLA proteins bind MYB5, reduce its association with GL2, and disrupt its cooperative interactions in the MBW/TTG2 module. 3. MYB5 overexpression enhances mucilage production and partially or completely rescues GA-deficient pectin and growth defects.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYB5 is a major seed-coat regulator, but its role in seedlings appears partly redundant with other MYB factors.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering pectin-rich traits or rescuing low-GA backgrounds; tissue specificity and redundancy should be considered.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation in MBW and TTG2-associated complexes; repressed by DELLA interaction
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell adhesion; wall integrity; seed mucilage production; seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive MBW component that enhances GL2 activation and TTG2 co-activation, thereby promoting pectin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core bHLH activator within the GA-DELLA-MBW-TTG2 regulatory module.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Cooperatively activates GL2 with TTG1/MYB5 and enhances TTG2-driven GL2 activation; DELLA weakens these interactions.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MBW component; interacts with TTG2 and TTG1; these interactions are hindered by GAI
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: RGL1; RGL2; RGL3; TTG1; MYB5; TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; split-LUC; co-IP; BiFC; Y3H; dual-LUC
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: GA3/PAC context inferred through DELLA abundance effects
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (IP-MS), PXD062288; public microarray datasets GSE12404; GSE20039
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EGL3 functions with TTG1 and MYB5 to activate GL2. 2. EGL3 also interacts with TTG2 to enhance TTG2-mediated GL2 activation. 3. DELLA proteins attenuate EGL3-containing interactions, reducing pectin-promoting transcriptional output.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EGL3 was studied mainly through interaction and reporter assays, not mutant phenotyping in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with MYB5/TTG2 for enhancing pectin biosynthesis, though direct whole-plant validation is limited.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of downstream pectin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: RG-I biosynthesis and broader pectin biosynthesis downstream of GA-DELLA-MBW-TTG2
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell adhesion; wall integrity; seed mucilage production; seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive downstream regulator of pectin biosynthesis; reduced in ttg1/ttg2 backgrounds and in GA-deficient conditions, while gl2-8 seedlings show reduced pectin content.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct transcriptional hub targeted by MBW proteins and TTG2 to mediate GA effects on pectin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates RG-I biosynthesis genes downstream to promote mucilage/pectin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: No protein complex requirement directly shown here; acts as shared transcriptional target of TTG2 and MBW module
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing siliques and seedlings; induced by GA3 in WT but not in ttg1-1, myb5-2, or ttg2-1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dual-LUC reporter assays using pGL2:LUC; ChIP-qPCR of MYB5 and TTG2 binding; mutant expression analysis; gl2-8 seedling phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Proteomics (IP-MS), PXD062288; public microarray datasets GSE12404; GSE20039
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: gl2-8 reduces seedling pectin but does not fully reproduce growth/cell adhesion defects, implying tissue-specific or redundant control.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GL2 is a direct target of both MYB5-containing MBW complexes and TTG2. 2. GA promotes, whereas DELLA represses, GL2 activation by modulating these upstream regulators. 3. GL2 contributes to pectin biosynthesis in both seed coat and seedlings, though its role in growth is not sufficient to explain all GA phenotypes.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although GL2 regulates downstream pathway genes, this paper primarily treats it as a transcriptional target and mediator rather than the top-level switch.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream node for enhancing pectin biosynthesis, but may need combination with parallel regulators to affect whole-seedling growth.
